title: Town Planning & Development Assessment: Skills Profile
description: Competency framework for statutory planning and development assessment practice in Tasmania.

skills.title: Statutory Planning & Legislation
skills.summary: Competence in navigating and interpreting planning legislation, local schemes, and regulatory frameworks.

competencies.title: Planning Scheme Interpretation
competencies.detail: Ability to read and apply zoning ordinances, overlays, and development codes (e.g., Hobart Interim Planning Scheme).

competencies.title: Statutory Compliance
competencies.detail: Understanding of state-specific environmental and planning acts (e.g., Land Use Planning and Approvals Act 1993 in Tasmania).

competencies.title: Delegated Authority
competencies.detail: Knowledge of statutory timeframes, permitted/discretionary application pathways, and exemption criteria.

skills.title: Development Assessment (DA)
skills.summary: Skills involved in evaluating development applications and balancing competing stakeholder interests.

competencies.title: Application Review
competencies.detail: Systematically assess application documents, architectural plans, and supporting technical reports (e.g., Traffic, Heritage, and Environmental Impact Assessments).

competencies.title: Conditions Drafting
competencies.detail: Ability to write clear, enforceable, and legally sound development permit conditions.

competencies.title: Referral Management
competencies.detail: Effectively coordinate and synthesize feedback from internal council departments (e.g., Engineering, Environmental Health) and external referral agencies.

skills.title: Strategic Planning & Policy
skills.summary: The ability to analyze data to support long-term urban growth and council visions.

competencies.title: Policy Formulation
competencies.detail: Contribute to the drafting, amendment, and review of local strategic plans and policy documents.

competencies.title: Spatial Analysis
competencies.detail: Proficiency in interpreting demographic, economic, and land-use data.

competencies.title: Urban Design Principles
competencies.detail: Evaluate built form, landscaping, and subdivision layouts for spatial efficiency, accessibility, and community amenity.

skills.title: Stakeholder Engagement & Communication
skills.summary: Managing relationships between developers, government, and the public.

competencies.title: Public Notification
competencies.detail: Facilitate and manage community consultation processes, handling public representations (objections/support) fairly and objectively.

competencies.title: Pre-Application Advice
competencies.detail: Conduct meetings with applicants and property owners to provide constructive feedback and de-risk project proposals.

competencies.title: Report Writing & Presentation
competencies.detail: Draft concise, impartial reports for council committees, elected members, or state planning commissions.

skills.title: Project & Time Management
skills.summary: Executing complex assessments within strict statutory timeframes.

competencies.title: Clock Management
competencies.detail: Monitor assessment clock deadlines (e.g., statutory 42-day assessment periods for discretionary applications).

competencies.title: Request for Further Information (RFI)
competencies.detail: Identify information gaps and draft clear, justified RFIs to applicants.

competencies.title: Conflict Resolution
competencies.detail: Mediate between competing interests (developers, residents, council vision) to reach optimal planning outcomes.
